{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.

Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.

In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 手塚光盛 | 画像 = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | 画像サイズ = 250px | 画像説明 = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛（東京都立図書館）歌川芳晴 | 時代 = [[平安時代]]末期 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 1184年 | 改名 = | 別名 =手塚太郎光盛 | 墓所 = | 主君 = [[源義仲]] | 氏族 = [[金刺氏]] | 父母 =父 手塚信綱(又太郎) 母 不詳 | 兄弟 = [[金刺盛澄]] | 夫 = | 子 = 熊王丸　[[唐糸姫]] }} '''手塚 光盛'''（てづか みつもり、? - [[寿永]]3年（[[1184年]]））は、[[平安時代]]末期の[[武将]]。[[諏訪神社]]下社の[[祝部]]である[[金刺氏]]の一族。手塚別当の子（甥とも）。兄に[[金刺盛澄]]。通称は太郎。 『[[源平盛衰記]]』では[[信濃国]][[諏訪郡]]の住人と記されているが、近年の研究により、居館跡や[[流鏑馬]]の遺構・光盛の菩提を弔った五輪塔や寺跡など手塚氏関連のものが多数残されている点、山梨県の大善寺に所蔵される三枝氏系図などにより、光盛をはじめとする手塚氏は[[上田市]]の[[手塚 (上田市)|手塚地区]]を本拠としていたと推定される上田市誌歴史編（4）「上田の荘園と武士」p.27-31（2001年）。しかしながら、現代において、上田市手塚地区に手塚姓の家系は存在しない。 石川県加賀市手塚町は手塚太郎光盛に因んで手塚町と付けられている。 == 経歴 == [[治承]]4年（[[1180年]]）、[[源義仲]]が挙兵するとその麾下に参加し、有力な部将の一人となる。[[寿永]]2年（[[1183年]]）、[[篠原の戦い]]に従軍。その際に[[斎藤実盛]]を討ち取った際の逸話は、『[[平家物語]]』において著名である。 [[平維盛]]の十万の軍勢は[[倶利伽羅峠]]の[[合戦]]で[[源義仲|源義仲（木曾義仲）]]軍に大敗北する。維盛は兵を引き[[加賀国]]の篠原で立て直すも[[篠原の戦い]]で敗北し総崩れとなる。[[斎藤実盛]]は老齢である事を隠す為に白髪を墨で黒く染め、平維盛に願い出て[[総大将]]が着る錦の直垂を着用し平家1騎のみで奮闘する。手塚光盛は名乗りを挙げるが相手は(お主を疑っているわけでは無いが考え有っての事、名を名乗る事は出来ない、近くに寄れ、戦おうぞ手塚。お前は日ノ本一の力自慢と組んだ事を名誉と思え。)討ち果たした光盛は不思議な平家の総大将の首を提げ義仲の陣に帰って来るなり不思議な戦いの経緯を説明した。 そして[[首実検]]も出来ずにいた。その首を[[樋口兼光]]に見せた、兼光はこの首には覚えがあるが実盛なら既に70歳を超えているはず。これを聞いた木曽義仲はもしやと思い近くの池で首を洗わせた。みるみると黒髪が白髪に変わった。名を名乗らない事も分かった、その人物の首は木曽義仲が産まれて2歳頃で命の恩人の斎藤実盛だった。『木曽義仲は人目もはばからず声を上げて泣いた。』 義仲の家臣も光盛も経緯が分かり涙したと言う。 寿永3年1月、[[源範頼]]、[[源義経|義経]]の追討軍と戦い、主君義仲と共に戦死。最後まで義仲に従った四騎の内の一人であったという。 近江国粟津（現在の滋賀県大津市）で討ち死にした。（[[粟津の戦い]]） [[平家物語]]、[[源平盛衰記]]では討死となっているが「義仲のかくし城」によると 手塚太郎光盛は怪我を負いながら今の大阪[[帝塚山]]まで逃げて来た。 帝塚山に数年滞在する内に嫁子をもうけ傷が癒え。信濃の木曾に向かい[[樋口兼光]]の遺児[[時兼]]と再会し[[観音堂]]を建立し義仲を弔った。手塚光盛は今の木曽町福島で[[西光寺]]を建立し住職を置き、今の松本市芳川にて[[村井城]]を築城し城主となるが、嫡子熊王丸に城主を譲り身を引く。その後、義仲の遺児の義重は仁科の養子になっており[[源義仲|源義仲（木曾義仲）]]の遺児、仁科義重の補佐をし今の長野県北安曇郡池田町の[[白駒城]]を築城し熊王丸と[[樋口時兼]]で固める。しかし、樋口時兼は失脚してしまう。 大阪府松原市城連寺・池内墓地 墓地中央に迎え地蔵堂がある。その手前に「十世孫手束太郎源光盛」と刻んだ墓石が祀られている。 手塚光盛の子孫は名を手塚から手束に変えて手塚光盛を供養している。 伝承 * 手塚太郎光盛の墓・長野県上田市手塚・長野県上田市諏訪形２９０７。 * 手塚太郎光盛の供養塔・大阪府松原市城連寺。 * 斎藤実盛の首を洗ったとされる池［首洗いの池］ 石川県加賀市手塚町 * 斎藤実盛の墓・ 石川県加賀市篠原 手塚光盛の晩年 *手塚光盛は今の木曽町日義に屋敷を構え余生を過ごした。 *諏訪で余生を過ごした。 *大阪の帝塚山に戻り余生を過ごした。 *上田塩田平にて余生を過ごした。 樋口時兼の子孫が 樋口行時である。 == 先祖子孫など == * 五世祖父：藤澤清直（次郎）、住 信州藤澤手塚家家系図 * 高祖父：藤澤義賢（次郎左衛門）、生 信州藤澤 * 曾祖父：手塚経賢（太郎）、生国 信州藤澤 * 祖父：手塚信澄（太郎） * 父：手塚信綱（又太郎）、生国 信州 * 子：手塚光清（小太郎）、生国 信濃 * 孫：手塚光行（太郎大夫） * 曾孫：手塚光親 * 後裔：手塚吉兵衛上田市安楽寺の手塚吉兵衛位牌内亜鉛板記述（著者：手塚良仙）『吉兵衛手塚府君金刺光盛之裔也』 * 後裔：手塚盛行（弥兵衛）、吉兵衛の子上田市安楽寺の手塚吉兵衛位牌内亜鉛板記述（著者：手塚良仙）『吉兵衛手塚府君金刺光盛之裔也其子盛行』。蘭医。水戸威公（[[徳川頼房]]）に仕えた。漫画家[[手塚治虫]]の9代前の先祖。 *[[歌舞伎]]『[[源平布引滝]]』の『[[実盛物語]]』では手塚光盛は[[源行綱]]と[[瀬尾兼氏]]の娘の間の子どもという設定となっており、母親が源氏の白旗を片手を犠牲にして守ったことから、[[斎藤実盛]]に源氏の白旗を守った片手のために塚を立て「手塚」を[[苗字]]とし、母の形見の[[日本刀|刀]]の銘にある「光盛」を名乗るが良いと言われる。 *[[御伽草子]]の『唐糸草子』には、光盛の死後における娘・唐糸と孫娘・万寿の身上が描かれている。 *小説家の[[海音寺潮五郎]]は、義仲の[[側室]]の一人・[[山吹御前]]は光盛の兄盛澄の娘である、との説を唱えている『[[平家物語]]』では、山吹は義仲の便女であって妻妾ではない。。 *後代において光盛の末裔を称する者に、[[江戸時代]]後期の[[蘭方医学|蘭学者]]・[[手塚光照|手塚良仙]]やその子孫である[[昭和]]の[[漫画家]]・[[手塚治虫]][http://tzk.cocolog-nifty.com/blog/2007/02/post_79fb.html Tezka Macoto' 6D -手塚眞ブログ-：SUWA]
[http://tzk.cocolog-nifty.com/blog/2007/01/post_80ee.html Tezka Macoto' 6D -手塚眞ブログ-：DALI]-「ウチの家系図がニセモノでなければ、シッカリ名前があるので、彼がご先祖になります」手塚治虫の代表作の一つ『[[火の鳥 (漫画)|火の鳥]]』・「乱世編」においては、手塚治虫の自画像そっくりに作画されている。などがいる。 *諏訪大社下社の金刺氏と上社の諏訪氏の系図には諸説あり、両氏の祖を同じとする説と別とする説とが存在する。藤沢氏については諏訪氏から分かれたとする有力な説があり、それに従えば手塚氏は諏訪氏一族という事になるが、近年においては手塚氏は金刺氏一族とする説がより一般的である。 * 上原榮治：「手塚太郎金刺光盛 ―手塚一族の後裔はどこへ？―」、グリーン美術出版（2015年7月）。※ 自費出版 * [http://mushimap.com/ueda-tezuka/ 「【長野県上田市】手塚太郎金刺光盛ゆかりの地」、虫マップ-手塚治虫ゆかりの地を訪ねて-] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てつか みつもり}} [[Category:平安時代の武士]] [[Category:金刺氏]] [[Category:手塚家|みつもり]] [[Category:源義仲]] [[Category:治承・寿永の乱で戦死した人物]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:1184年没]]

後裔：手塚盛行（弥兵衛）、吉兵衛の子上田市安楽寺の手塚吉兵衛位牌内亜鉛板記述（著者：手塚良仙）『吉兵衛手塚府君金刺光盛之裔也其子盛行』。蘭医。水戸威公（[[徳川頼房]]）に仕えた。漫画家[[手塚治虫]]の9代前の先祖。 *[[歌舞伎]]『[[源平布引滝]]』の『[[実盛物語]]』では手塚光盛は[[源行綱]]と[[瀬尾兼氏]]の娘の間の子どもという設定となっており、母親が源氏の白旗を片手を犠牲にして守ったことから、[[斎藤実盛]]に源氏の白旗を守った片手のために塚を立て「手塚」を[[苗字]]とし、母の形見の[[日本刀|刀]]の銘にある「光盛」を名乗るが良いと言われる。 *[[御伽草子]]の『唐糸草子』には、光盛の死後における娘・唐糸と孫娘・万寿の身上が描かれている。 *小説家の[[海音寺潮五郎]]は、義仲の[[側室]]の一人・[[山吹御前]]は光盛の兄盛澄の娘である、との説を唱えている『[[平家物語]]』では、山吹は義仲の便女であって妻妾ではない。。 *後代において光盛の末裔を称する者に、[[江戸時代]]後期の[[蘭方医学|蘭学者]]・[[手塚光照|手塚良仙]]やその子孫である[[昭和]]の[[漫画家]]・[[手塚治虫]][http://tzk.cocolog-nifty.com/blog/2007/02/post_79fb.html Tezka Macoto' 6D -手塚眞ブログ-：SUWA]
[http://tzk.cocolog-nifty.com/blog/2007/01/post_80ee.html Tezka Macoto' 6D -手塚眞ブログ-：DALI]-「ウチの家系図がニセモノでなければ、シッカリ名前があるので、彼がご先祖になります」手塚治虫の代表作の一つ『[[火の鳥 (漫画)|火の鳥]]』・「乱世編」においては、手塚治虫の自画像そっくりに作画されている。などがいる。 *諏訪大社下社の金刺氏と上社の諏訪氏の系図には諸説あり、両氏の祖を同じとする説と別とする説とが存在する。藤沢氏については諏訪氏から分かれたとする有力な説があり、それに従えば手塚氏は諏訪氏一族という事になるが、近年においては手塚氏は金刺氏一族とする説がより一般的である。 * 上原榮治：「手塚太郎金刺光盛 ―手塚一族の後裔はどこへ？―」、グリーン美術出版（2015年7月）。※ 自費出版 * [http://mushimap.com/ueda-tezuka/ 「【長野県上田市】手塚太郎金刺光盛ゆかりの地」、虫マップ-手塚治虫ゆかりの地を訪ねて-] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てつか みつもり}} [[Category:平安時代の武士]] [[Category:金刺氏]] [[Category:手塚家|みつもり]] [[Category:源義仲]] [[Category:治承・寿永の乱で戦死した人物]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:1184年没]]

{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 手塚光盛 | 画像 = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | 画像サイズ = 250px | 画像説明 = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛（東京都立図書館）歌川芳晴 | 時代 = [[平安時代]]末期 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 1184年 | 改名 = | 別名 =手塚太郎光盛 | 墓所 = | 主君 = [[源義仲]] | 氏族 = [[金刺氏]] | 父母 =父 手塚信綱(又太郎) 母 不詳 | 兄弟 = [[金刺盛澄]] | 夫 = | 子 = 熊王丸　[[唐糸姫]] }} '''手塚 光盛'''（てづか みつもり、? - [[寿永]]3年（[[1184年]]））は、[[平安時代]]末期の[[武将]]。[[諏訪神社]]下社の[[祝部]]である[[金刺氏]]の一族。手塚別当の子（甥とも）。兄に[[金刺盛澄]]。通称は太郎。 『[[源平盛衰記]]』では[[信濃国]][[諏訪郡]]の住人と記されているが、近年の研究により、居館跡や[[流鏑馬]]の遺構・光盛の菩提を弔った五輪塔や寺跡など手塚氏関連のものが多数残されている点、山梨県の大善寺に所蔵される三枝氏系図などにより、光盛をはじめとする手塚氏は[[上田市]]の[[手塚 (上田市)|手塚地区]]を本拠としていたと推定される上田市誌歴史編（4）「上田の荘園と武士」p.27-31（2001年）。しかしながら、現代において、上田市手塚地区に手塚姓の家系は存在しない（引越により2020年現在1世帯）。 石川県加賀市手塚町は手塚太郎光盛に因んで手塚町と付けられている。 == 経歴 == [[治承]]4年（[[1180年]]）、[[源義仲]]が挙兵するとその麾下に参加し、有力な部将の一人となる。[[寿永]]2年（[[1183年]]）、[[篠原の戦い]]に従軍。その際に[[斎藤実盛]]を討ち取った際の逸話は、『[[平家物語]]』において著名である。 [[平維盛]]の十万の軍勢は[[倶利伽羅峠]]の[[合戦]]で[[源義仲|源義仲（木曾義仲）]]軍に大敗北する。維盛は兵を引き[[加賀国]]の篠原で立て直すも[[篠原の戦い]]で敗北し総崩れとなる。[[斎藤実盛]]は老齢である事を隠す為に白髪を墨で黒く染め、平維盛に願い出て[[総大将]]が着る錦の直垂を着用し平家1騎のみで奮闘する。手塚光盛は名乗りを挙げるが相手は(お主を疑っているわけでは無いが考え有っての事、名を名乗る事は出来ない、近くに寄れ、戦おうぞ手塚。お前は日ノ本一の力自慢と組んだ事を名誉と思え。)討ち果たした光盛は不思議な平家の総大将の首を提げ義仲の陣に帰って来るなり不思議な戦いの経緯を説明した。 そして[[首実検]]も出来ずにいた。その首を[[樋口兼光]]に見せた、兼光はこの首には覚えがあるが実盛なら既に70歳を超えているはず。これを聞いた木曽義仲はもしやと思い近くの池で首を洗わせた。みるみると黒髪が白髪に変わった。名を名乗らない事も分かった、その人物の首は木曽義仲が産まれて2歳頃で命の恩人の斎藤実盛だった。『木曽義仲は人目もはばからず声を上げて泣いた。』 義仲の家臣も光盛も経緯が分かり涙したと言う。 寿永3年1月、[[源範頼]]、[[源義経|義経]]の追討軍と戦い、主君義仲と共に戦死。最後まで義仲に従った四騎の内の一人であったという。 近江国粟津（現在の滋賀県大津市）で討ち死にした。（[[粟津の戦い]]） [[平家物語]]、[[源平盛衰記]]では討死となっているが「義仲のかくし城」によると 手塚太郎光盛は怪我を負いながら今の大阪[[帝塚山]]まで逃げて来た。 帝塚山に数年滞在する内に嫁子をもうけ傷が癒え。信濃の木曾に向かい[[樋口兼光]]の遺児[[時兼]]と再会し[[観音堂]]を建立し義仲を弔った。手塚光盛は今の木曽町福島で[[西光寺]]を建立し住職を置き、今の松本市芳川にて[[村井城]]を築城し城主となるが、嫡子熊王丸に城主を譲り身を引く。その後、義仲の遺児の義重は仁科の養子になっており[[源義仲|源義仲（木曾義仲）]]の遺児、仁科義重の補佐をし今の長野県北安曇郡池田町の[[白駒城]]を築城し熊王丸と[[樋口時兼]]で固める。しかし、樋口時兼は失脚してしまう。 大阪府松原市城連寺・池内墓地 墓地中央に迎え地蔵堂がある。その手前に「十世孫手束太郎源光盛」と刻んだ墓石が祀られている。 手塚光盛の子孫は名を手塚から手束に変えて手塚光盛を供養している。 伝承 * 手塚太郎光盛の墓・長野県上田市手塚・長野県上田市諏訪形2907。 * 手塚太郎光盛の供養塔・大阪府松原市城連寺。 * 斎藤実盛の首を洗ったとされる池［首洗いの池］ 石川県加賀市手塚町 * 斎藤実盛の墓・ 石川県加賀市篠原 手塚光盛の晩年 *手塚光盛は今の木曽町日義に屋敷を構え余生を過ごした。 *諏訪で余生を過ごした。 *大阪の帝塚山に戻り余生を過ごした。 *上田塩田平にて余生を過ごした。 樋口時兼の子孫が 樋口行時である。 == 先祖子孫など == * 五世祖父：藤澤清直（次郎）、住 信州藤澤手塚家家系図 * 高祖父：藤澤義賢（次郎左衛門）、生 信州藤澤 * 曾祖父：手塚経賢（太郎）、生国 信州藤澤 * 祖父：手塚信澄（太郎） * 父：手塚信綱（又太郎）、生国 信州 * 子：手塚光清（小太郎）、生国 信濃 * 孫：手塚光行（太郎大夫） * 曾孫：手塚光親 * 後裔：手塚吉兵衛上田市安楽寺の手塚吉兵衛位牌内亜鉛板記述（著者：手塚良仙）『吉兵衛手塚府君金刺光盛之裔也』 * 後裔：手塚盛行（弥兵衛）、吉兵衛の子上田市安楽寺の手塚吉兵衛位牌内亜鉛板記述（著者：手塚良仙）『吉兵衛手塚府君金刺光盛之裔也其子盛行』。蘭医。水戸威公（[[徳川頼房]]）に仕えた。漫画家[[手塚治虫]]の9代前の先祖。 *[[御伽草子]]の『唐糸草子』には、光盛の死後における娘・唐糸と孫娘・万寿の身上が描かれている。 *小説家の[[海音寺潮五郎]]は、義仲の[[側室]]の一人・[[山吹御前]]は光盛の兄盛澄の娘である、との説を唱えている『[[平家物語]]』では、山吹は義仲の便女であって妻妾ではない。。 {{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.

In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.

Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | Image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | Image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>

'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>

In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

{{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.

He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. {{Infobox Japanese Samurai | Name = Tezuka Mitsumori | image = 武勇高名組討撰 手塚太郎 斉藤実盛.jpg | image size = 250px | Caption = "Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori and Saitō Sanemori" by Utagawa Yoshiharu (Tokyo Metropolitan Library) | Era = Late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth date = Unknown | Death date = 1184 | Former name = | Other names = Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori | Burial site = | Lord = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}} | Clan = {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} | Parents = Father: Tezuka Nobutsuna (Matatarō), Mother: Unknown | Siblings = {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}} | Spouse = | Children = Kumaōmaru, {{ill|Tōkarito Hime|ja|唐糸姫}} }}
'''Tezuka Mitsumori''' (手塚 光盛, ? - {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184)) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, who served as ritualists of the {{ill|Shimosha|ja|下社}} of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. He was also known as Tezuka Betto's son (or nephew) and had an elder brother, {{ill|Kanemasa Morizumi|ja|金刺盛澄}}. His common name was Tarō.
In the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, Mitsumori is identified as a resident of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Suwa District|ja|諏訪郡}}. Recent studies suggest that the Tezuka clan, including Mitsumori, was based in the {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}} of {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}} due to the presence of relics such as a residence site, {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} structures, a five-story stupa, and temples connected to Mitsumori’s legacy.<ref>Ueda City Historical Records (2001), "Manors and Samurai of Ueda," p. 27-31.</ref> However, no descendants of the Tezuka clan currently reside in the area. Tezuka-chō in {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|石川県加賀市}} is named after Tezuka Tarō Mitsumori.
== Career == In {{ill|Jishō|ja|治承}} 4th year (1180), when Minamoto no Yoshinaka raised an army, Mitsumori joined as a prominent commander. In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 2nd year (1183), he participated in the {{ill|Battle of Shinohara|ja|篠原の戦い}}. An episode from this battle is notable in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|ja|平家物語}}, where Mitsumori defeated {{ill|Saitō Sanemori|ja|斎藤実盛}}.
Sanemori, concealing his old age by dyeing his white hair black, fought valiantly in the colors of the general {{ill|Taira no Koremori|ja|平維盛}}. After a fierce duel, Mitsumori brought Sanemori's head back to Yoshinaka's camp. When the head was washed, the dye faded, revealing white hair. Recognizing the man as his savior during childhood, Yoshinaka wept openly.<ref>The Tale of the Heike.</ref>
In {{ill|Juei|ja|寿永}} 3rd year (1184), during the {{ill|Battle of Awazu|ja|粟津の戦い}}, Mitsumori fought against the armies of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, dying alongside his lord Yoshinaka. He was one of the last loyal retainers to Yoshinaka.
== Descendants and Legacy == Tezuka Mitsumori is associated with several legends and remains: * His grave is located in {{ill|Tezuka area|ja|手塚 (上田市)}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|ja|上田市}}. * The Tezuka clan changed their surname to Tetsuka and established a memorial for Mitsumori in Osaka's {{ill|Tezukayama|ja|帝塚山}}.
Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲}}

Mitsumori's lineage is said to include: * Tezuka Mitsuharu, a descendant who served as a {{ill|rangaku|ja|蘭学|12=draft}} scholar. * {{ill|Osamu Tezuka|ja|手塚治虫|12=draft}}, a famous manga artist, is believed to be a 9th-generation descendant.<ref>Osamu Tezuka’s work "Phoenix" ("Civil War Chapter") references his lineage.</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲|12=draft}}
